content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :    Barbara Milman                                    Date    :    May 29, 1981


From    :    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
             Judy Davis

Subject:    Opinion Request Meeting May 26, 1981
             Definition of Commercial Lending Institution

             The consensus of the meeting was that a real estate
broker or brokerage firm is not a "commercial lending
institution" as used in 82030(8)(A).

             Other facts which may be material should the FPPC
ever seek to draft a regulation defining such are con-
tained on page 2 of a letter to Tom Hutchins, Deputy D.A.
of Ventura County, dated May 28, 1981.

             Areas of law which may be relevant to such a regula-
tion:

             1.    Those "institutional lenders" the federal
government insures or which are eligible for such
insurance.

                  a)    banks - FDIC
                  b)    savings and loans - FSLIC
                  c)    credit unions - NCUAB

             2.    Those lenders subject to "truth in lending" laws.

             3.    Those lenders licensed by law specifically to
lend - pawnbrokers, personal property brokers, etc.

             Helpful numbers:

                  Department of Real Estate -        Barbara Leidigh-    3-2143
                  Calif. Assn. of Realtors -        Ron Kingston    -444-2045
                  Sacramento Board of Realtors - Tom Bannon      -444-6010


JD:sf

sections.conclusion: The consensus of the meeting was that a real estate broker or brokerage firm is not a 'commercial lending institution' as used in §2030(A).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a real estate broker or brokerage firm qualifies as a 'commercial lending institution' under Political Code Section 82030(A).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that real estate brokers and brokerage firms do not qualify as 'commercial lending institutions' under Section 82030(A). The opinion identifies relevant legal criteria for defining such institutions, including federal insurance eligibility, truth-in-lending law applicability, and specific state licensing requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be internal memorandum rather than formal advice letter; lacks formal CONCLUSION section header but contains clear consensus statement; some text quality issues from scanning; references external letter for additional factual material; provides helpful reference contacts and relevant legal frameworks (federal insurance programs, truth-in-lending laws, specific licensing requirements).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a real estate broker or brokerage firm qualifies as a 'commercial lending institution' under Political Code Section 82030(A).

CONCLUSION: The consensus of the meeting was that a real estate broker or brokerage firm is not a 'commercial lending institution' as used in §2030(A).

embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum from an Opinion Request Meeting dated May 25, 1981, addressing whether real estate brokers or brokerage firms constitute 'commercial lending institutions' under Political Code Section 82030(A). The FPPC concluded they do not meet this definition and provided relevant legal frameworks and contact information for further research.
